description: Database layer: db_manager.py (SQLAlchemy connection manager with MySQL/SQLite fallback), models.py (all 6 table definitions as SQLAlchemy ORM models), and migrations.py (idempotent auto-create tables script). Also update requirements_v2.txt with all new dependencies and update .env.example with MySQL config.
steps: Create J.A.R.V.I.S_system/core/database/__init__.py with module docstring and exports

Create J.A.R.V.I.S_system/core/database/db_manager.py: DatabaseManager class with connection pooling via SQLAlchemy create_engine. Support MySQL (pymysql driver) with fallback to SQLite. Read config from env vars (DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, DB_NAME, DB_FALLBACK_SQLITE). Include get_session(), get_engine(), auto-reconnect logic. Wrap sqlalchemy import in try/except - if unavailable, class still loads but methods return error dicts.

Create J.A.R.V.I.S_system/core/database/models.py: Define SQLAlchemy ORM models for 6 tables: Task (with priority/status enums), Knowledge, Conversation, Journal, Habit, HabitLog. Use declarative_base(). Include proper relationships (HabitLog -> Habit FK). Match the exact SQL schema from the spec.

Create J.A.R.V.I.S_system/core/database/migrations.py: Script with create_all_tables() function that calls Base.metadata.create_all(engine). Idempotent (checkfirst=True is default). Print status of tables created. Include if __name__ == '__main__' block for direct execution.
Update J.A.R.V.I.S_system/requirements_v2.txt: Add sqlalchemy>=2.0.0, pymysql>=1.1.0, pandas>=2.0.0, duckduckgo-search>=5.0.0, scikit-learn>=1.3.0, requests>=2.31.0 with section comments
Update J.A.R.V.I.S_system/.env.example: Add MySQL section (DB_HOST=localhost, DB_PORT=3306, DB_USER=root, DB_PASSWORD=, DB_NAME=jarvis_db, DB_FALLBACK_SQLITE=true)
acceptance_criteria: python -c 'from core.database.db_manager import DatabaseManager' succeeds without error (run from J.A.R.V.I.S_system/)
python -c 'from core.database.models import Task, Knowledge, Conversation, Journal, Habit, HabitLog' succeeds
DatabaseManager falls back to SQLite when MySQL is unavailable (no crash)
verification: cd /projects/sandbox/PRE-AIAgentJarvis/J.A.R.V.I.S_system && pip install sqlalchemy pymysql 2>/dev/null; python -c "from core.database import db_manager, models, migrations; print('Database layer OK')"

cd /projects/sandbox/PRE-AIAgentJarvis/J.A.R.V.I.S_system && python -c "from core.database.db_manager import DatabaseManager; dm = DatabaseManager(); print('Fallback:', dm.engine.url if hasattr(dm, 'engine') and dm.engine else 'graceful')"
findings: SQLAlchemy 2.0+ DeclarativeBase requires __allow_unmapped__ = True when using Column() with type annotations. SQLite fallback works correctly. The existing test_command fails due to missing openai module - this is pre-existing and not caused by database changes. pyenv global 3.11.15 was needed to set Python version.
